events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist Brian May was born on this day in 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! The Queen legend continues to inspire generations with his iconic guitar riffs.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger was born on this day in 1943.

angles.name: Still Rolling
angles.copy: 83 years young and still bringing the moves! Happy birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger, rock's most enduring frontman.

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) officially launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the very first music video aired.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Remember when MTV first hit our screens? 45 years ago today, 'Video Killed The Radio Star' kicked off a revolution. Did it, though?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' in 1994
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring classics like 'Live Forever' and 'Supersonic'.

angles.name: Britpop Debut
angles.copy: 32 years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the game. Pure Britpop brilliance from start to finish. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began in 1969
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, billed as 'An Aquarian Exposition: 3 Days of Peace & Music', kicked off on August 15, 1969.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years since half a million gathered for three days of peace and music. The spirit of Woodstock lives on.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on this day in 1977; he would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us 49 years ago today. His music and legacy are eternal.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, an influential figure in hip-hop, died on September 13, 1996, from injuries sustained in a shooting; he would have been 55.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: 30 years ago, we lost a true icon. Remembering Tupac Shakur and his profound impact on music and culture.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Guitar virtuoso Jimi Hendrix passed away on September 18, 1970; he would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Remembering Jimi
angles.copy: Remembering Jimi Hendrix, who left us 56 years ago today. His innovative guitar playing continues to resonate.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' in 1969
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic 'Abbey Road' album, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover, was released in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: 57 years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still one of the most famous photos in music history.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman Chrissie Hynde was born on this day in 1951.

angles.name: Rock Goddess
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon and a voice that demands attention.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Former Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher was born on this day in 1972.

angles.name: Legendary Frontman
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that swagger and those anthems.

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' single in 1971
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971.

angles.name: A Song For Peace
angles.copy: 'Imagine' all the people living life in peace... 55 years since John Lennon released his powerful message to the world.

events.title: Apple announced the first iPod in 2001
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, promising '1,000 songs in your pocket', with the device shipping on November 10.

angles.name: Music In Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before streaming? 25 years ago, Apple announced the original iPod, changing how we listen to music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th marks James Bond Day, celebrating the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Who's your favourite Bond and what's the best Bond theme tune? Let us know!

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Legendary Beatles co-founder John Lennon was born on this day in 1940.

angles.name: Remembering a Visionary
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the incomparable John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese was born on this day in 1939.

angles.name: Comedy Legend
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, a true master of comedy.

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' in 1968
events.blurb: The Beatles released their self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: Double Album Classic
angles.copy: 58 years since The Beatles unleashed 'The White Album'. A sprawling, diverse masterpiece that still sparks debate.
